source_name: European Banking Authority
title: EBA updates Pillar 3 disclosure requirements on ESG risks, equity and shadow banking exposures, as part of simplification effort
clean_title: EBA updates Pillar 3 disclosure requirements on ESG risks, equity and shadow banking exposures, as part of simplification effort
summary: The European Banking Authority (EBA) has published today its final draft Implementing Technical Standards (ITS) amending the Pillar 3 disclosure framework on environmental, social and governance (ESG) risks, and introducing disclosure requirements on equity and shadow banking exposures. The package finalises the implementation of the disclosure requirements introduced by the Capital Requirements Regulation (CRR 3). Developed in line with the EU’s simplification agenda and the Omnibus package, the ITS streamline existing requirements, and enhance usability and consistency.
why_selected: Thématique réglementation ; source jugée fiable (european banking authority) ; impact réglementaire.
impacts: Peut modifier la conformité, les reportings ou la gouvernance des acteurs financiers.
title_signature: disclosure eba effort equity esg exposures part pillar requirements risks
normalized_title: eba updates pillar 3 disclosure requirements on esg risks equity and shadow banking exposures as part of simplification effort

source_name: European Banking Authority
title: Latest EBA MREL dashboard shows that MREL requirements range from 25% to 29% of risk-weighted assets, depending on bank category, while bail-in remains the preferred resolution strategy
clean_title: Latest EBA MREL dashboard shows that MREL requirements range from 25% to 29% of risk-weighted assets, depending on bank category, while bail-in remains the preferred resolution strategy
summary: The European Banking Authority (EBA) today published its latest semi-annual dashboard on the minimum requirement for own funds and eligible liabilities (MREL), providing an update on the state of resolution planning and on the resources that banks are using to meet their requirements. As of December 2025, bail-in remains the preferred resolution strategy in terms of risk-weighed assets (RWAs), while rollover needs reach EUR 231 billion for instruments set to become ineligible over the next 12 months.
why_selected: Thématique finance ; source jugée fiable (european banking authority) ; impact marché.
impacts: Peut affecter les conditions de marché, les valorisations ou l’allocation d’actifs.
title_signature: assets bail category dashboard depending eba latest mrel preferred range
normalized_title: latest eba mrel dashboard shows that mrel requirements range from 25 to 29 of risk weighted assets depending on bank category while bail in remains the preferred resolution strategy
